contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert kW to HP?
contentBlocks.html:
Kilowatt (kW) is the SI unit of power. Horsepower (HP) is a traditional unit used for engine power. There are several definitions: metric HP (PS) = 0.7355 kW, mechanical HP = 0.7457 kW. This converter uses metric HP.

This conversion is essential for car specifications (European vs US power ratings), electric motor specifications, generator sizing, and HVAC equipment.

Examples: 100 kW = 136 HP, 150 kW = 204 HP, 200 kW = 272 HP. A Tesla Model 3 Standard Range has about 208 kW = 283 HP.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.description: The conversion result appears instantly in the field next to it – no clicking required.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

contentBlocks.title: How does kW to HP conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
1 metric horsepower (PS) = 75 kgf·m/s = 735.499 W = 0.7355 kW. Therefore, 1 kW = 1/0.7355 = 1.3596 HP.

Note: mechanical (imperial) HP = 745.7 W = 0.7457 kW, giving 1 kW = 1.341 HP. The difference between metric and mechanical HP is about 1.4%.

To convert kW to metric HP, multiply by 1.3596. To convert HP to kW, multiply by 0.7355.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• City car: 55–75 kW = 75–102 HP. Family car: 100–150 kW = 136–204 HP.
  • Sports car: 200–400 kW = 272–544 HP. Supercar: 500+ kW = 680+ HP.
  • EU vehicle registration certificates list power in kW.
  • 1 HP (metric) = 0.9863 HP (mechanical). The difference is negligible for most purposes.

items.question: How many HP is 1 kW?
items.answer: 1 kW = 1.3596 metric HP (PS) or 1.341 mechanical HP.

items.question: How many kW is 200 HP?
items.answer: 200 metric HP = 147.1 kW. 200 mechanical HP = 149.1 kW.

items.question: What is the difference between HP and PS?
items.answer: PS (Pferdestärke) is metric horsepower = 735.5 W. HP (mechanical) = 745.7 W. PS is about 1.4% less than mechanical HP.

items.question: How much HP does a typical car have?
items.answer: City car: 75–100 HP. Family car: 130–200 HP. Sports car: 250–500 HP.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
